author: peterchen-intel
title: smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest primType is unexpected: unknown_i32 Expected acl_i32 on ARM64
signature: primtype is unexpected unknown expected acl|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: CPU functional tests / CPU functional tests
failed_jobs.symptom: 1 test failed: smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest primType is unexpected : unknown_i32 Expected : acl_i32

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_ubuntu_22_arm64_cross_compilation
failed_jobs.symptom: Overall status check failed due to upstream test job failure

key_errors:
src/plugins/intel_cpu/tests/functional/utils/cpu_test_utils.cpp:248: Failure
Value of: primTypeCheck(primType)
  Actual: false
Expected: true
primType is unexpected : unknown_i32 Expected : acl_i32
root_cause: A single ARM64 CPU functional test (smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic) failed because the ACL (ARM Compute Library) primitive was not selected for an i32-to-i8 Convert operation. The test expects 'acl_i32' but received 'unknown_i32'. This failure occurred in a run triggered by PR #36202, which is a GPU-only change (MoE expert offloading), with no modifications to ARM64 CPU plugin code or ACL kernel registration. The mismatch between expected and actual primitive type is consistent with an intermittent ACL kernel selection issue on the ARM64 runner. Out of 71,986 smoke tests, only this one failed, suggesting a flaky or environment-sensitive condition rather than a code regression introduced by the PR.
reproduction_steps:
Run on an ARM64 machine with ACL support:
./ov_cpu_func_tests --gtest_filter='smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic/ConvertCPULayerTest.CompareWithRefs/IS=[?.?.?.?]_TS=(2.4.4.1)_(2.17.5.4)_(1.2.3.4)_(1.3.3.3)_inputPRC=i32_specialValue=none_targetPRC=i8__inFmts=nchw_outFmts=nchw_primitive=acl'
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs to confirm whether this is a transient flaky test.
If the failure recurs, investigate ARM64 ACL kernel registration for i32 Convert in src/plugins/intel_cpu/src/nodes/convert.cpp.
Check if ACL supports i32 as input precision for Convert operations in the current ACL version used in the ARM64 build.
Add the test to the expected_failures skip config at tests/functional_test_utils/layer_tests_summary/github/skip_configs/CPU/expected_failures_OP.csv if confirmed as a known intermittent failure.
prevention_strategies: Monitor ACL kernel coverage for all precision combinations in the ARM64 CPU plugin. Ensure ACL fallback behavior is properly handled when a primitive is not supported, and distinguish between 'not supported' (should skip) vs 'should use ACL but didn't' (should fail).
historical_context: No prior occurrences of this specific failure pattern found in the CI Doctor MQ database.
